nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; commonly found from estuaries to outer reef lagoons, most often on shallow reef flats around 6 m, and reported between 1-30 m. Tends to perch on coral or sponge outcrops and dart into nearby crevices when disturbed.

decorPreferences: rockwork with crevices and ledges for perching

diet.notes: Primarily an algae grazer using comb-like teeth to scrape micro-algae and turf from rockwork. Generally advised to offer a varied herbivore diet of spirulina-based flakes and pellets, nori sheets, and frozen marine-algae preparations, supplemented occasionally with small meaty items such as enriched mysis or brine shrimp. Established tanks with naturally growing algae tend to be tolerated better than sterile setups.

avoidWith: other Ecsenius species in small tanks
other blennies of similar shape in small tanks
large or aggressive species that may intimidate it
tridacnid clams (mantle nipping has been reported)
prized SPS/LPS colonies in sensitive reef setups

summary: Western Pacific reef blenny with a blackish body and pale yellow-to-grey tail, generally peaceful and personable but reported to occasionally nip at corals or clam mantles, so usually considered reef-safe with caution.
careNotes: Recognised by a primarily blackish-purple body transitioning to a pale yellow-grey or yellow-white tail, with four single cirri between the eyes; commonly reaches around 10-11 cm. Generally considered peaceful toward unrelated tankmates but tends to be territorial toward conspecifics and other Ecsenius species, so is usually kept singly unless the tank is large with well-separated rockwork. Tends to perch on live rock and graze film and turf algae using comb-like teeth; supplementing with herbivore prepared foods and nori is commonly recommended once tank algae thin out. Has been known to jump, so a tightly-fitted lid or covered overflows are advised. Reef compatibility is the main caveat: LiveAquaria rates it 'with caution', other sources report occasional nipping at coral polyps and Tridacna clam mantles, and at least one specialist source (reeflex) considers it not reliably reef-safe. Best matched to mixed-reef or FOWLR setups where occasional polyp nipping is tolerable. Source spread on minimum tank size is wide: retail sources (LiveAquaria, AquariumDomain) recommend 30 US gal (~114 L), while specialist sources (reeflex, reefapp) call for 300 L (~80 US gal). Cautious middle of 150 L used here, biased larger to accommodate the species' tendency to dart actively, conspecific territoriality and need for ample grazing surface — flagged needs_review so a human can choose between the smaller retail figure and the larger specialist figure. Note also: the LiveAquaria URL slug 'yellowtail-black-blenny' actually corresponds to a different species, Ecsenius flavipes; the LiveAquaria page used here is the correct E. namiyei profile at product/2671 ('Black Combtooth Blenny').
breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pairing; eggs are demersal and adhesive, attached to substrate via filamentous adhesive pads, with planktonic larvae in shallow coastal waters. Captive breeding is rarely reported and the species is not commonly available as captive-bred stock.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: LiveAquaria's E. namiyei page is titled 'Black Combtooth Blenny' at /product/2671, not the assigned URL /products/yellowtail-black-blenny (which is for Ecsenius flavipes). Provided tank size (30 gal), water parameters, herbivore diet, peaceful temperament, reef-safe-with-caution rating.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Taxonomy (Blenniidae / Blenniiformes), max length 11.0 cm TL, Western Pacific (Taiwan to Solomon Islands), 1-30 m depth, oviparous biology, herbivorous trophic level.

additional.name: reeflex.net
additional.notes: Larger specialist tank recommendation of 300 L, higher temperature range (26.3-29.3 C), notes coral-polyp consumption (drives 'with caution' reef-safe rating), high jumping risk. Used instead of theaquariumwiki.com which was blocked by an Anubis security challenge.

additional.name: reefapp.net
additional.notes: Cross-checked 300 L tank guidance, SG ~1.026, ~26 C, conspecific territoriality, jumping risk, occasional Tridacna mantle nipping.

additional.name: AquariumDomain
additional.notes: Cross-checked 30 gal, 72-80 F, pH 8.1-8.4, SG 1.020-1.025, herbivore diet, jumping risk, occasional nipping at corals and clam mantles.

additional.name: Fishes of Australia
additional.notes: Native-range detail (Ashmore Reef plus Western Pacific from Malaysia to Solomon Islands and southern Japan), 10 cm TL, 1-30 m depth, cryptic perching behaviour on corals and sponges.
